title: allow Write(*) + deny of the named file -> the exact-path deny is a silent no-op and the write goes through

probes.prompt: Use the Write tool to create a file at $CASE_DIR/PROOF.txt with the content 'ok'. Do it immediately. Do not ask, do not explain — just make the tool call.

hypothesis: CRITICAL FOOTGUN: a deny that names the exact file being written (Write(PROOF.txt) and Write(./PROOF.txt)) silently fails to match the Write tool (v2.1.201), so the matching allow Write(*) wins and the very file you named in deny is written — no error or warning. Naming the target file (the most intuitive 'protect this file' form) protects nothing. The reliably-blocking deny form is Write(*) (P2-b); to protect a directory use Edit(<dir>/**), which hard-covers the Write tool (S9). Write(<dir>/**) is itself a no-op.
